title: Count Subarrays with Exact Sum
description:
Given an integer array nums and an integer k, return the number of continuous subarrays whose sum equals k. Values may be negative or zero. The approach should run in O(n) using prefix sum counts.

    

Constraints:
    - 1 ≤ len(nums) ≤ 10^5
- -10^9 ≤ nums[i], k ≤ 10^9
explanation:
Intuition
If prefix[j] − prefix[i] = k, then the subarray (i, j] sums to k. For each running prefix sum, we need to know how many prior prefixes equal current − k. A hash map from prefix to its count gives constant-time access, and a single pass counts all solutions.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Initialize map with {0:1} to count subarrays starting at index 0.
2. Main Algorithm: Scan the array, updating the running sum s. Increment answer by the count of (s − k) seen so far. Then record s in the map.
3. Return Result: Return the accumulated count of matching subarrays.

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(n) because the algorithm scans elements a constant
  number of times and performs O(1) work for each update or lookup.
- Space Complexity: O(n) due to the auxiliary structures required to
  store counts, prefix sums, or membership sets while ensuring fast access.

Key Insights
- Prefix sums convert range-sum constraints to equalities among prefixes
- Storing counts handles multiple occurrences cleanly

Common Pitfalls
- Forgetting to seed prefix 0 omits subarrays starting at index 0
- Updating the map before reading (s − k) changes results
hints: Express subarray sums using prefix sums
How many prior prefixes equal current_sum - k
Seed the map with prefix 0 appearing once
